sizing_rule.note: Reinvest profits to scale position size proportionally: New Position = Base Position × (Cumulative Equity / Initial Equity). Example: 5000 × (115/100) = 5750. This is equity-multiplied position scaling after profitable trades (anti-martingale principle with compounding effect).

source_rule_text: Reinvestment of profits into trading capital increases position size on subsequent trades, reducing average trade return but demonstrating the compounding effect of scaling positions with accumulated equity.
